subject: Toucan Signal design-token compliance (globals.css + i18n type escapes)
context: web-audit: globals.css:31-37,51 has token drift — --color-primary and --color-accent are DIFFERENT hues; Toucan Signal (D-014, dudoxx-design-system.md §9a) requires --color-primary = --color-accent = ONE terracotta, plus a cobalt --color-link axis (= --color-ring / --color-info), green = success-only. globals.css:64-67 has a DEAD --xterm-*-hex mirror block orphaned by appearance.ts — DELETE it (appearance.ts owns the raw-hex xterm theme, the ONE sanctioned exemption; do NOT touch appearance.ts values). Add a Bricolage Grotesque display face via next/font/google as --font-display (keep Inter for UI/body + JetBrains Mono for terminal data — the three-face stack). AppearanceControls.tsx:94,138 uses `t(`...` as never)` to escape i18n typing — replace with a typed keyof-union key (build a const map of allowed keys typed as a union, so t() resolves without `as never`). Verify: no gradients, no container borders (shadow-elev-N only), semantic @theme tokens throughout, in BOTH light and dark. This shard is parallel-safe with the WS4->WS1->WS3 chain (touches only globals.css + AppearanceControls, not routing or xterm-client).
steps: Read web-audit.md + globals.css:31-67 to see the primary/accent split, the cobalt gap, and the dead --xterm-*-hex block.

Unify --color-primary and --color-accent to the ONE Toucan Signal terracotta OKLCH (hues within +-2). Add a cobalt --color-link token aliased to --color-ring / --color-info per §9a. Keep green as success-only.
Delete the dead --xterm-*-hex mirror block (globals.css:64-67). Confirm appearance.ts is unaffected (it is the real xterm theme source; leave its raw hex untouched).
Declare Bricolage Grotesque via next/font/google, expose it as --font-display in globals.css / the layout font wiring; keep Inter (UI) + JetBrains Mono (data).
In AppearanceControls.tsx replace both `t(... as never)` escapes with a typed keyof-union key map so t() type-checks with zero `as never` and zero any.
Grep the src tree for raw palette utilities / gradients / container-border separators (excluding appearance.ts) and replace any with semantic tokens / shadow elevation.
Run web typecheck/lint/test; visually confirm light+dark parity.
pitfalls: FM3: NEVER rewire the xterm canvas theme to the new OKLCH @theme tokens — xterm ITheme cannot parse OKLCH -> blank/black terminal. appearance.ts raw hex is the ONE sanctioned exemption; leave it untouched and only remove the DEAD globals.css mirror.
Bricolage must load via next/font/google (self-hosted), not a raw <link> (avoids layout shift + CSP issues).
Removing `as never` must not reintroduce any — use a typed keyof union, not a cast.
No gradients, no container borders as separators (shadow-elev-N only) — design-system §25.
validation: pnpm --filter ddx-term-web typecheck && pnpm --filter ddx-term-web lint && pnpm --filter ddx-term-web test
acceptance_criteria: WHEN globals.css tokens are resolved THE SYSTEM SHALL have --color-primary and --color-accent at the same terracotta hue (within +-2) and a cobalt --color-link present (AC2.1, AC2.2).
WHEN the display face is inspected THE SYSTEM SHALL declare Bricolage as --font-display alongside Inter and JetBrains Mono (AC2.3).
WHEN globals.css is scanned THE SYSTEM SHALL contain no --xterm-*-hex mirror block (AC2.4).
WHEN components/term is scanned THE SYSTEM SHALL contain no `as never` i18n escapes and no raw palette utilities/gradients/container borders outside appearance.ts (AC2.5, AC2.6).
WHEN the theme is toggled THE SYSTEM SHALL render correctly in both light and dark with the xterm canvas intact (AC2.7).
